title: Crispy Fermented Brazilian Rice Flour Pancakes
description: A crispy and flavorful solo dinner dish featuring fermented rice flour pancakes, served with a nimono simmer, this recipe is macrobiotic friendly and cooked to perfection on a raclette grill.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the fermented rice flour, water, coconut oil, salt, and sugar to form a smooth batter.
Heat the raclette grill to medium high heat and brush with a small amount of coconut oil.
Using a 1/4 cup measuring cup, scoop the batter onto the grill and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
While the pancakes are cooking, heat the nimono simmer sauce in a small saucepan over low heat.
Once the pancakes are cooked, place them on a plate and brush with the warm nimono simmer sauce.
Sprinkle the chopped scallions and sesame seeds over the top of the pancakes.
Repeat the cooking process until all the batter is used up, about 8-10 pancakes.
Keep the cooked pancakes warm in a low temperature oven while cooking the remaining batter.
